title: From Single Images to Motion
concept: Text-to-video generation extends image generation into the time dimension, producing a coherent sequence of frames that flow smoothly together.

exercises.prompt: What do you think makes a VIDEO different from a single picture?

options.text: A video is just many pictures shown quickly, one after another
A video is a picture with sound only
A video is a picture drawn bigger

exercises.explain: That's it! A video is really just lots of still images (frames) played fast so they look like they're moving.

cards.title: What if a picture could move?
cards.body: You've seen AI make a single cool image from a text prompt. But what if you asked for a *video* instead? Suddenly the AI needs to imagine not just one moment, but a whole story unfolding across time. That's a much bigger challenge - and it's happening right now!

cards.title: Text-to-Video Generation
cards.body: Text-to-video generation is when an AI creates a short video clip from a text description. It builds on the same ideas as text-to-image AI, but now it must make MANY frames that connect smoothly, so the motion looks natural instead of jumpy or flickery.

cards.title: Like a Flipbook Artist
cards.body: Imagine an artist drawing a flipbook. Each page is a slightly different picture, and when you flip through fast, it looks like the character is walking or jumping. Text-to-video AI is like a super-fast flipbook artist that draws every single page so they connect perfectly into smooth motion.

cards.title: A Dog Running on the Beach
cards.body: You type: 'a golden retriever running along a sunny beach.' A text-to-image AI would give you ONE frozen moment of the dog running. A text-to-video AI gives you several seconds of the dog's legs moving, sand kicking up, and waves rolling - all flowing together like real motion.

cards.title: Myth: It's Just Stitched Pictures
cards.body: Myth: The AI just makes random separate pictures and glues them together. Truth: The AI plans the frames TOGETHER so objects stay consistent - the dog doesn't change color, the beach doesn't disappear. It thinks about time and motion, not just one image at a time.

cards.title: Image AI vs Video AI
cards.body: Both start from a text prompt, but they solve very different puzzles.

left.text: Creates ONE frozen frame that matches the prompt. No motion, no time to worry about.

right.text: Creates MANY connected frames over time, keeping objects consistent as they move smoothly.

exercises.prompt: What is the main new challenge that text-to-video AI has to solve compared to text-to-image AI?

options.text: Keeping many frames consistent and smooth over time
Choosing a font for text on screen

exercises.explain: Correct! Video AI must generate a sequence of frames that flow together smoothly, unlike image AI which only makes one still picture.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A video is basically many still images shown quickly one after another.
exercises.explain: True! That's exactly what a video is - a fast sequence of frames (like a flipbook) that our eyes see as smooth motion.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentence.
exercises.sentence: Text-to-video AI extends text-to-image ideas into the ___ dimension, creating a sequence of frames.
exercises.explain: Video adds the dimension of TIME - the AI must generate many frames that change smoothly across seconds, not just one static image.

exercises.prompt: Which clip was most likely made by a text-to-video AI, rather than just being a single AI image?

options.text: A 4-second clip of a cat's tail swishing and ears twitching naturally over time
One still photo of a cat sitting on a windowsill
A single painting of a cat in a garden

exercises.explain: Right! A clip with motion happening smoothly over several seconds is the sign of video generation - not just one frozen picture.

exercises.prompt: Boss Challenge: Why can't a text-to-image AI simply be used to make a video by generating a few random images of the same prompt?

options.text: Because random separate images won't stay consistent or flow smoothly - the AI needs to plan frames together across time
Because text-to-image AI can only make black and white pictures
Because images can never show animals or people
Because video needs sound and images never have sound

exercises.explain: Exactly! Random separate images might show different colors, shapes, or backgrounds each time. Real video AI generates connected frames that move smoothly and stay consistent, capturing motion across time.
